questions.statement:
<p><strong>Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word.</strong></p>

<p>The designer created an&nbsp;<u>intricate</u>&nbsp;pattern for the dress.</p>

<p><strong>Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>TRANSPARENT&nbsp;</p>

<p><strong>&nbsp;Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.</strong></p>

<p>Conceited</p>

<p><strong>Direction: Select the most appropriate word for the given group of words.</strong></p>

<p>A small structure with one or more open sides that is used to vend merchandise</p>

<p style=""><span style="font-weight: 700;">Find the most appropriate one-word for the given sentence.</span></p>

<p style="">A doctor who studies and treats the medical conditions and diseases of women</p>

<p>Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence.</p>

<p>The eldest sister received <u>the lion&rsquo;s share</u> of her father&rsquo;s property.&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>In the following question, four words are given out of which one word is correctly spelt. Select the correctly spelt word.</strong></p>
questions.explanation:
<p>The correct answer is&nbsp;<strong>option 2</strong>.</p>

<p>Malicious means&nbsp;characterized by malice; intending or intended to do harm.</p>

questions.statement:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks.<br />
He is going to ________ the campaign against excessive use of ________ in local production units.

Select the word that best completes the given sentence. Determine the collocation through the sentence.<br />
Marz would describe it in ___________ detail.

<p dir="ltr"><strong>In the following question, one part of the sentence has an error. Read the sentence to find the error and mark the corresponding option. If the sentence has no error choose option 4 &lsquo;No error&rsquo; as your answer.</strong></p>
On our trip to Neer Garh waterfall, my younger brother started acting down (A) because our parents decided for us to walk the rest of the journey, (B) which was roughly four kilometres long, instead of renting a car. (C) No error (D)
